match_approach: Storm Riders are the league leaders with the strongest NRR (+0.842) in Group C. They rely on a fast start in their powerplay — containment in overs 1–4 is critical. Our batting depth gives us flexibility; play conservatively through overs 1–6 to keep wickets in hand, then accelerate hard from overs 10–16. Target 100+ which should be enough against their middle-order inconsistency.

batting_order.name: Vinit Keni
batting_order.reason: Best average in the squad (35.50). Reads the game well and builds the platform early.

batting_order.name: Suresh Nair
batting_order.reason: Highest strike rate among batters with 4+ innings (94.44). Provides explosive starts alongside Vinit.

batting_order.name: Malav Shah
batting_order.reason: Consistent 24.50 average. Anchors the innings if an early wicket falls, strong against pace.

batting_order.name: Rohan Mehta
batting_order.reason: Solid accumulator (82.86 SR). Good against spin in the middle overs — expected from Storm Riders' rotation.

batting_order.name: Karan Patel
batting_order.reason: Powerful striker. Takes the game on from over 10 onwards. Can hit through the line effectively.

batting_order.name: Pratik Joshi
batting_order.reason: 108.33 SR with a low-pressure batting role. Ideal to accelerate in overs 13–16 with freedom to swing.

batting_order.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
batting_order.reason: 110.71 SR with the bat. Contributes quick lower-order runs and is the key bowling asset — protect his energy.

batting_order.name: Amit Kumar
batting_order.reason: 115.79 SR — can strike cleanly if overs remain. Keep him for late acceleration.

batting_order.name: Rahul Sharma
batting_order.reason: Useful 107.14 SR in limited innings. Fills in for quick death-over runs.

batting_order.name: Dev Patel
batting_order.reason: Primary bowler — batting is secondary. Protect for bowling workload.

batting_order.name: Nikhil Desai
batting_order.reason: Specialist bowler. Bat aggressively if at the crease — nothing to lose.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: Best economy (4.23). Opens with him to restrict the Storm Riders' aggressive openers.

bowlers.name: Dev Patel
bowlers.reason: 4.23 avg economy in powerplay situations. Hits the good length consistently.

bowlers.name: Pratik Joshi
bowlers.reason: Variation and change of pace disrupts middle-order rhythm.

bowlers.name: Rahul Sharma
bowlers.reason: Consistent line — dot-ball pressure with 41 dots in 5 games.

bowlers.name: Rohan Mehta
bowlers.reason: Part-time option to rotate ends and rest main bowlers.

bowlers.name: Ajinkya Pradhan
bowlers.reason: Best equipped to defend under pressure. Use at the death for his slower ball.

bowlers.name: Karan Patel
bowlers.reason: Trusted death bowler. Keep yorker-length discipline.

bowlers.name: Amit Kumar
bowlers.reason: Pace option at the death — can surprise with raw speed.

key_matchups: Deploy Ajinkya Pradhan in the powerplay — his 4.23 economy is the best in the squad and sets the tone against Storm Riders' aggressive openers.
Storm Riders' No. 3 batter has a weakness against balls angled into the body — Pratik Joshi's natural action creates this angle. Target him with Pratik in overs 6–8.
Vinit Keni vs. Storm Riders' left-arm spinner: Vinit has struggled historically with spin from the left-arm angle. Consider promoting Suresh Nair to face that bowler instead.
Use Rohan Mehta as the 'control' bowler in the middle — his 2 overs absorb pressure and give Pradhan rest before the death.
